originalTitle: Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee
title: Great News for Our African Referee!
content:
Hello, Friends! Guess What Happened!

Hello, my amazing friends! Do you know what makes news exciting? It's when we hear stories about people doing great things, especially from our own beautiful continent, Africa! Today, I have a really happy story to tell you about a very special man from Somalia. Somalia is a lovely country in East Africa, with beautiful beaches and warm people. This story is all about fairness and dreams coming true, even when things don't go exactly as planned. Get ready to hear about our football hero, Omar Artan!

Meet Omar Artan: Our Super Referee from Somalia!

Once upon a time, not so long ago, there was a wonderful man named Omar Artan. He lives in Somalia, a bright and sunny country. Omar is not a football player, but he is super important for football games! He is a referee. Can you say "referee"? It's a special word for a very important job. Omar worked very hard and became one of the best referees in the whole world! He was so good that he was chosen to go to the biggest football party ever – the World Cup! Isn't that amazing? To be chosen from so many people is a huge honor. He made his country, Somalia, and all of Africa very proud!

What is a Referee Anyway?

"Did you know?" A referee is like the head teacher of a football game! Imagine a game of football with your friends. Sometimes, people might disagree about what happened, right? That's where the referee comes in! The referee wears a special uniform and runs all around the field. Their job is to make sure everyone follows the rules of the game. They blow a whistle, show yellow or red cards, and make sure everything is fair. They help the players play safely and correctly. It's a very big responsibility! Omar Artan is one of these amazing people who make sure football is fun and fair for everyone. He has to be very brave and make quick decisions!

The Amazing World Cup!

Now, let's talk about the World Cup! "Did you know?" The World Cup is the biggest football tournament in the whole wide world! It happens only once every four years. Countries from all over the world send their best football teams to play against each other. It's like a huge party where everyone celebrates football! This time, the World Cup was going to be held in a country far away called the United States of America. Imagine all the excitement! Millions and millions of people watch these games on TV. And guess what? Our very own Omar Artan was chosen to be one of the referees for this grand event! He was going to help make sure the games were fair on the world stage.

A Little Problem on the Way

But then, something a little sad happened. Omar Artan was all ready to pack his bags and go to the United States. He had his whistle, his cards, and his special referee uniform. He was so excited to represent Somalia and Africa! But, to visit another country, you sometimes need a special paper called a "visa." This paper lets you enter that country. Sadly, Omar was not able to get his visa for the United States. This meant he could not travel there. It was a big disappointment for him and for everyone who was cheering for him. He worked so hard to get ready, but he just couldn't go. Imagine how he must have felt!

Who is FIFA? And What Did They Do?

"Did you know?" There is a very important group called FIFA. FIFA is like the boss of all international football. They organize big football tournaments, like the World Cup, and they make sure football rules are the same everywhere. They are the ones who chose Omar Artan to be a referee! When FIFA heard that Omar couldn't get his visa and couldn't go to the World Cup, they knew they had to do something fair. Omar had worked so hard, he was ready, and it wasn't his fault that he couldn't travel. So, FIFA decided to do a very kind and fair thing.

Good News for Omar!

And here is the happy part of our story! Even though Omar Artan could not go to the United States and referee the games at the World Cup, FIFA decided that he would still get all his money! This money is called a "tournament fee." It's like getting paid for a job you were going to do. "Did you know?" Even if he wasn't there, FIFA still paid him the full amount, just as if he had been blowing his whistle on the field! This is wonderful news for Omar and his family. It shows that hard work is always valued, and fairness is very important. He prepared for months, he trained, he studied the rules – he did his part.

Why This is Super Important!

This story is very important for all of us, especially for kids like you! It shows that even when unexpected things happen and plans change, good people and good organizations try to do the right thing. FIFA's decision to pay Omar Artan in full means they respected his hard work and dedication. It teaches us about fairness, honesty, and kindness. It also reminds us that people from Africa, like Omar, are among the best in the world in many things, including sports! He is a role model for many children in Somalia and all over Africa who dream of becoming referees or football players.

Keep Dreaming Big!

So, my young friends, what a happy ending to a tricky situation! Omar Artan, our super referee from Somalia, is still a hero. He might not have gone to the World Cup, but he is still recognized for his amazing talent and hard work. This story tells us to never give up on our dreams, to work hard, and to always believe in fairness. Who knows, maybe one day, one of you will be the next great referee or football star from Africa, making our continent proud at the biggest sports events in the world! Keep playing, keep learning, and keep dreaming big!
summary: Omar Artan, a talented football referee from Somalia, will still receive his full payment from FIFA for the World Cup even though he could not travel to the United States to officiate the games.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that hard work is valued, fairness matters, and a big organization like FIFA supports its referees, even when unexpected problems stop them from doing their job.

originalTitle: Author Chimamanda Adichie accuses hospital of stalling review into son's death
title: A Sad Story and a Big Question for a Famous Writer
content:
Hello, Friends!

Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here. Today, we have a story that might sound a little bit sad, but it's also about finding answers and making things fair. It's about a very famous lady from our continent, Nigeria.

Who is Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie?

First, let's meet our special person. Her name is Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie. That's a big name, isn't it? But she is a very important lady from Nigeria, which is a big country in Africa. She is like a magical storyteller! She writes amazing books and stories that people all over the world love to read. Imagine someone who can paint beautiful pictures with words. That's Chimamanda! Did you know? Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! It's a land of many cultures, languages, and delicious foods. Lagos, where our story takes place, is one of its biggest cities, full of bustling markets and lively streets.

A Very Sad Day

Now, for the sad part of our story. Chimamanda had a little boy. He was very, very young, just 21 months old. That means he was almost two years old, still a tiny toddler, learning to run and play and say his first words. One day, this little boy got sick and went to a hospital in Lagos. Hospitals are special places where doctors and nurses help people get better when they are sick or hurt. But something very sad happened. Chimamanda's little boy did not get better. He passed away at the hospital. This made his mommy, daddy, and everyone who loved him very, very sad. It’s okay to feel sad when you hear about something like this.

What is a Hospital For?

Let's talk a little more about hospitals. They are very important buildings in our towns and cities. Inside, you find super-smart doctors and super-kind nurses. They wear special uniforms and work very hard. Their job is to make sick people feel better and help them get healthy. They give medicine, take care of wounds, and sometimes even do special operations. They want everyone to be healthy and strong. Did you know? Many doctors and nurses in Africa work incredibly hard every day to care for lots of people. They are like real-life superheroes, helping our communities stay well!

Asking Important Questions

When something sad like this happens, especially in a hospital, people often want to know exactly what happened. It’s like when you lose your favorite toy, and you try to remember where you last saw it. You ask questions to find answers. In grown-up language, this process is called an "inquest." Can you say "in-quest"? An inquest is like a special meeting or a big puzzle that grown-ups try to solve together. The goal of an inquest is to find out all the facts. They ask: What exactly happened? Why did it happen? Could anything have been done differently? It's all about understanding what took place.

Chimamanda Wants Answers

Chimamanda, like any mommy, wants to know exactly what happened to her precious little boy. She wants to understand every single detail. This is very natural when you lose someone you love. She believes that the hospital is making it very difficult for this special meeting, this "inquest," to happen. She says it's like they are trying to stop the puzzle from being solved or the questions from being asked. She feels that the hospital is trying to keep the review from moving forward. A "review" means looking very carefully at everything that happened. She just wants the truth to come out, so she can understand. Did you know? When families lose someone they love, knowing what happened can help them feel a little bit better, even though their hearts are still sad. It helps them to understand the situation.

Why Finding Answers Matters

Why is it so important to find these answers? Well, when we understand what happened, it can help make sure it doesn't happen to other children. Hospitals can learn from what happened and become even better and safer places. It's also about fairness. Everyone deserves to know the truth, especially when something very sad and important happens. It helps people feel that things are being handled correctly and openly. Imagine if you lost your favorite drawing, and no one helped you look for it. You would feel a bit sad and maybe a little unfair, wouldn't you? This is much, much bigger than a drawing, but the feeling of wanting answers is similar.

What Happens Next?

So, what will happen now? Chimamanda hopes that the special meeting, the inquest, will finally be allowed to happen. She hopes that all the important questions will be asked carefully. She wants all the facts to come out, so everyone can understand what happened on that very sad day. She wants fairness for her little boy and for her family. We hope that everyone involved will work together to find the answers. Because knowing the truth is important for healing, for learning, and for making sure our hospitals are the best they can be for everyone. Did you know? Being a good hospital means always learning and trying to do things better. Asking questions and finding answers helps them grow stronger and safer for all patients. It's a very serious situation, and we hope for a fair and clear outcome for Chimamanda and her family. We will keep you updated, my young news friends!
summary: A famous Nigerian writer, Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie, is very sad because her young son passed away in a hospital, and she says the hospital is making it hard for her to find out exactly what happened.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows how important it is for everyone to get fair answers when something sad happens, especially when it involves hospitals, so that we can learn and make sure everyone is safe and treated well.

originalTitle: Kidnapped Nigerian retired general dies in captivity
title: Learning About Safety and Helpers in Nigeria
content:
A Brave Helper

Hello, super friends! We have some news from Nigeria, a wonderful country in Africa. Sometimes, we hear stories that are a little bit sad, but they help us learn important things about staying safe and helping each other. Imagine a very brave grown-up who works hard to keep people safe. His name was General Rabe Abubakar. He was like a big leader in the army, a group of brave men and women who help protect the country. General Rabe was a special person who cared a lot about Nigeria and its people. He helped make important plans to keep everyone safe and sound. He was a retired general, which means he had worked for a very long time and was now enjoying his rest.

What is a General?

Did you know that a 'general' is a very important leader in the army? They are like the captain of a big team, making sure everyone knows their job to help protect their country and its people. They wear special uniforms and have lots of training. They learn how to plan, how to be brave, and how to look after many people. General Rabe was one of these amazing helpers. He had helped Nigeria for many, many years, making sure families could live in peace. It’s a big job, like being the head teacher of a very, very big school, but for safety!

What Happened to General Rabe?

Sometimes, even in places like Nigeria, there are grown-ups who do not follow the rules and make bad choices. These grown-ups can cause problems for others. General Rabe was taken away from his home by some people who did not ask for permission. We call this 'kidnapped'. It means someone is taken away when they don't want to go, and it makes their family and friends very worried. It's a sad thing when someone is taken like that. Sadly, after some time, we learned that General Rabe would not be coming home. This made many people in Nigeria very sad. Everyone wished he could have come back safely to his family and friends.

Why Do Things Like This Happen?

This kind of story reminds us that sometimes, there are 'security challenges' in certain places. This is a grown-up way of saying that some areas might have problems where things are not always safe. It means that some people might not feel safe because of bad people who cause trouble. These bad people might take things that are not theirs, or they might try to hurt others. But it's very important to remember that many, many good grown-ups, like the police, the army, and community leaders, are working super hard every single day to make sure these places become safe again for everyone, especially for children. Did you know? Nigeria is a very big country with many different kinds of people and beautiful places! It has the largest population in Africa, with over 200 million people!

Working Towards a Safer Home

The story of General Rabe helps us remember that keeping everyone safe is a very important job for all grown-ups. Leaders in Nigeria are working hard to stop bad things from happening. They want to make sure everyone can go to school, play outside, and live without fear. They are making new plans and asking people to work together. This is called building a stronger community. When everyone helps each other, it's harder for bad people to cause problems. They want to make sure that no more people are taken away and that everyone feels protected. It’s like when everyone in your class works together to keep the playground clean and safe.

What Can We Do?

Even though you are still young, you can help make your community a safer place too! Always listen to your parents or trusted grown-ups. They are your best helpers. If you ever see something that makes you feel scared or worried, always tell a trusted adult. It could be your mom, dad, teacher, or another grown-up you know and trust very much. They will know what to do. Did you know? Sharing your worries with a trusted adult is a very brave thing to do! It helps them keep you and others safe.

Looking to a Brighter Future

Even with sad news, we always have hope for a brighter future. Leaders in Nigeria and all over Africa are working hard to make sure that every child can grow up in a safe and happy home. They are teaching everyone how important it is to be kind, to follow rules, and to help each other. When everyone works together, our homes, schools, and countries become happier and safer places. We remember General Rabe as a brave helper who cared deeply about his country. His story reminds us to keep working hard for peace and safety, and to always look out for each other. Let's all be helpers, just like General Rabe! Did you know? Many children in Nigeria love to play football, just like many children around the world! Working as a team is important in sports and in keeping communities safe!
summary: General Rabe Abubakar, a brave retired army leader from Nigeria, sadly passed away after being taken from his home, reminding us that grown-ups are working hard to solve safety problems and keep everyone safe in Africa.
whyMatters: This story helps us remember that staying safe is very important, and many brave grown-ups are working hard to protect everyone, especially children, in places like Nigeria.

originalTitle: Thousands protest as Trump, other world leaders set to meet for G7 summit
title: Thousands protest as Trump, other world leaders set to meet for G7 summit
content: Activists rally in Geneva to denounce policies of G7 countries ahead of group's annual meeting this week in France.

originalTitle: Al Jazeera reports from Israeli attack site in southern Beirut
title: Hello from Africa! Let's Talk About News and Being Safe
content:
Hello, everyone! It's me, your friendly news reporter from warm, sunny Africa! I'm here to share some news with you today. News helps us learn about the world. It tells us what is happening far away. And sometimes, it tells us about important things that happen closer to home too. Today, we are talking about a special kind of reporting. We are talking about when something big happens. My job is to tell you about it in a way that is easy to understand. We want you to feel safe and sound. We also want you to know that grown-ups are always working to help.

Hello from Your African News Friend!

It's me, your news friend! I love telling stories from all over the world. Even from places far, far away from our beautiful Africa. Sometimes, we hear about things that make us curious. Sometimes, we hear about things that make us think. Today, my reporter friends and I are looking at a place. It's a place called Beirut. Beirut is a big city. It's very far from Africa, across a big blue sea. In Beirut, something happened. There was a big noise. And now, my job is to tell you what we see and what we hope for. We are here to see what happened. We are here to listen to people. We are here to tell you the story. But we will tell it in a gentle way. We want you to know that grown-ups are helping. They are making sure everyone is okay.

What Happened in Beirut?

Well, friends, in a place far away, there was a big event. It happened in a city called Beirut. In this big city, there are many tall buildings. These buildings are where people live. They are called residential buildings. Think of them like your home, or your friend's home, but maybe much taller! **Did you know?** A residential building is just a big house where many families live! Like a giant block of homes stacked up. One of these buildings had a big problem. There was a loud sound. And after the loud sound, things changed a little bit. That's why reporters like me go to see. We want to understand what happened. We want to see how we can help. Or, how grown-ups are helping already. We are not going to talk about scary things. We are going to talk about helpers. We are going to talk about being safe. And we are going to talk about wishing for peace. That's what grown-ups do when big things happen.

Our Job as Reporters

My job as a reporter is super important! I get to go to places and see things. Then, I tell you what I see. It's like being a detective, but for news! I look for clues. I listen to what people say. I make sure my story is true. **Did you know?** A news reporter's job is to share true stories with people, so they know what's happening in the world! When something big happens, like in Beirut, my job is to go there. I look around carefully. I see what needs to be fixed. I see who is helping. I tell you about the good things happening, even when something tough has happened. We wear special vests sometimes. They help people know we are reporters. We have cameras to take pictures. We have microphones to record sounds. All of this helps us tell the story. We want to show you the world. We want to show you how people are strong and kind.

Seeing Helpers Everywhere!

When we arrived at the place, what did we see? We saw many, many helpers! Helpers are super important. They come to make everything better. They come to help people. They come to clean up. We saw people wearing bright uniforms. These might be the police. The police help keep everyone safe. They make sure people don't go to places that are not safe yet. They guide people. We also saw people who looked like doctors or nurses. They help anyone who might be hurt. They give bandages and kind words. They are very brave! **Did you know?** There are many kinds of helpers in the world, like doctors, firefighters, police officers, and even kind neighbours! And there were regular people too. Just like you and me! They were helping their neighbours. Maybe they were sharing some water. Maybe they were helping clean up a little bit. It shows that people are very good. They care about each other. This is what we saw the most. We saw many hands helping. We saw many kind faces. It made us feel hopeful. Even when something big and loud happens, people help each other.

What Does "Safe" Mean?

When something big happens, the most important thing is for everyone to be safe. "Safe" means you are not in danger. It means you are protected. It means you are away from anything that can hurt you. The grown-ups at this place are making sure everyone is safe. They are telling people where to go. They are making sure buildings are strong. They are checking everything very carefully. It's important for all of us to listen to grown-ups. Grown-ups know best how to keep you safe. If they say "stay here" or "don't touch that," they are doing it to protect you. **Did you know?** Listening to grown-ups is one of the best ways to stay safe, especially when big things are happening around you! Being safe means you can play. Being safe means you can learn. Being safe means you can sleep soundly at night. That's what we want for all the kids in Beirut. And for all the kids everywhere in the world, including our amazing kids in Africa!

Wishing for Peace

After big things happen, everyone hopes for peace. What is peace? Peace means no fighting. Peace means everyone is friends. Peace means people can live happily together. It means everyone feels safe and calm. We reporters, from Africa and everywhere, wish for peace. We want everyone to live without big loud noises. We want all children to play and laugh. We want all grown-ups to smile. **Did you know?** Peace means a time when people are kind to each other and there is no fighting. It's a wonderful feeling for everyone! Our hearts are with the people in Beirut. We hope that everything gets fixed soon. We hope that all the big buildings are safe again. We hope that everyone can go back to their regular happy lives. From our African home, we send our warmest wishes. We send wishes for strength. We send wishes for calm. And most of all, we send wishes for peace. Peace for Beirut, peace for Africa, and peace for our whole beautiful world. Thank you for listening to my news report today. Remember, be kind, be safe, and let's all wish for peace together!
summary: A kid-friendly news reporter from Africa shares news about a big event in Beirut, focusing on the amazing helpers, the importance of staying safe, and wishing for peace for all children.
whyMatters: It's important to know what happens in the world, and that even after big problems, many people help each other. We can all wish for a safe and peaceful world for every child, no matter where they live.

originalTitle: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap, early projections say
title: Big News from Far Away! Switzerland Says 'Welcome, Friends!'
content:
Hello, amazing friends and super learners all across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, Zola the Zebra, here with some exciting news from a land far, far away. Imagine a place with tall, snowy mountains, yummy chocolate, and lots of happy cows with bells. That special place is called Switzerland!

What Was the Big Question?

In Switzerland, grown-ups had a very important question to answer. It was like deciding something big for your classroom or your home. The question was: 'Should our country have a special number limit for how many people can live here?' Think of it like a school bus. If a bus has seats for 50 children, should we only let 50 children on, no more? Or is it okay for more people to come and make new friends? This idea of a 'limit' is called a 'population cap'. 'Population' just means all the people living in a place. So, a 'population cap' means putting a stop to how many people can live in a country. Some people thought, 'Maybe we should stop at 10 million people.' That's a super big number, like ten times a million! Can you imagine that many people? *Did you know?* Switzerland is a small country in Europe, but it's very famous for its beautiful nature and delicious cheese!

How Do Grown-Ups Make Big Choices?

So, how do people in Switzerland make such a big decision? They vote! 'Voting' is like when you raise your hand to choose a game to play with your friends, or when you pick which storybook to read in class. But for grown-ups, it's a very serious way to choose. They go to a special place, or sometimes even do it from home, and they mark a paper or press a button to say 'yes' or 'no' to an idea. Every grown-up gets to share what they think is best for their country. It's how everyone's voice can be heard. *Did you know?* Many countries in Africa also use voting to choose their leaders and make important decisions!

What Did the Swiss People Choose?

Guess what happened? The grown-ups in Switzerland voted! They looked at the big question: 'Should we have a limit on how many people can live here?' And a lot of people, more than half of them, said 'NO!' This means they 'rejected' the idea of a limit. 'Rejected' is just a grown-up word for saying 'no, thank you' to something. So, the people of Switzerland decided that they *don't* want to stop new people from coming to live in their beautiful country. They want to keep welcoming everyone! *Did you know?* When more than half the people say 'yes' or 'no', that's usually how big decisions are made in many places around the world!

Why Did They Say 'Welcome'?

Why do you think they said 'no' to a limit? Well, maybe they thought that having more people means more friends, more ideas, and more fun! When new people come to a country, they can bring new games, new songs, new foods, and new ways of doing things. It can make a country even more exciting and colorful! Think about your own classroom or your village. If a new friend comes to your school, do you want to say, 'Sorry, no more space!' Or do you want to say, 'Come on in! Let's play and learn together!' Most of us love to welcome new friends, right? It's often the same feeling for a whole country. *Did you know?* People from all over the world live together in many countries, sharing their cultures and making everything richer and more interesting!

What Does This Mean for Switzerland?

So, because the grown-ups voted 'no' to the limit, Switzerland will continue to be a place where people can come and live. It means that the country can keep growing, not just in size, but with new families, new workers, and new children who will learn and play there. It shows that the people of Switzerland believe in being open and friendly. This news reminds us that making big choices is important. And sometimes, the best choice is to be kind and welcoming. Just like we welcome friends and visitors in our homes and communities here in Africa, the people of Switzerland decided to keep their doors open. They chose kindness and friendship for their country's future.

A Message of Friendship

This story from Switzerland shows us that even in countries far away, people think about being good neighbors and welcoming friends. It's a happy story about choosing to share and grow together. Isn't that wonderful? We learn that everyone, no matter where they live, wants a good place to be. It's a wonderful example of how people can make a big decision with their votes, and choose to make their country a welcoming place for everyone. This helps keep the world a friendly and happy place for all of us. So, remember this story, my friends! When people vote, they are making a big choice for their community. And in Switzerland, they chose to say a big 'WELCOME!' That's all the news for today from your African reporter, Zola the Zebra. Keep learning and keep smiling!
summary: People in Switzerland voted against a plan to limit the number of people who can live in their country, choosing instead to welcome more friends.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that people can make big decisions together through voting, and they chose to be welcoming and friendly to everyone who wants to live in their country.

content: Ben Gannon-Doak wasn't born when Scotland last played at the men's World Cup. But the 20-year-old was at the heart of their long-awaited return and their first win at the tournament in 36 years as he stole the show against Haiti.

originalTitle: BBC Sport pundits choose England World Cup XIs - who would you pick?
title: BBC Sport pundits choose England World Cup XIs - who would you pick?
content: Thomas Tuchel will pick his first England World Cup team on Wednesday, when the Three Lions take on Croatia. BBC Sport pundits choose their starting XIs - who would you pick?
